items.title: PM sidesteps question on whether house prices will continue falling — as it happened
items.summary: Anthony Albanese has sidestepped questions about whether house prices will continue falling, following the release of data today showing a 0.4 per cent drop in home values.

items.title: Australia hammers West Indies to claim a spot in the Women's T20 World Cup cricket final
items.summary: Australia makes it through to another Women's T20 World Cup final, after a scary moment when West Indies star Deandra Dottin collapsed during the national anthems.

items.title: NT teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible despite crackdown
items.summary: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

items.title: Beechworth doctors revert to home visits for patients after taxi service left town
items.summary: When their local taxi service folded 18 months ago, Beechworth residents struggled to get to their medical appointments.

items.title: New research reveals Queensland's skin cancer burden, triggers calls for more prevention campaigns
items.summary: Middle-aged Queenslanders undergo almost 1.5 million skin cancer procedures a year, according to one of the studies.

items.title: Life-changing HIV medication made free in SA in step welcomed by advocates
items.summary: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

items.title: Australia to play Poland in first Davis Cup tie held in Brisbane in almost a decade
items.summary: The countries will face off in a 2026 World Group I tie at the Pat Rafter Arena on September 19 and 20.

items.title: Real-world incidents targeting Jews reinforce 'spiral of hate' online, royal commission hears
items.summary: The royal commission has heard new research which found anti-Jewish hate increased online both after October 7, 2023 and the Bondi Beach terror attack in December.

items.title: Conversion practice ban legislation set to be debated in Tasmanian parliament
items.summary: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home
items.summary: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

items.title: From talking sheep to incel horror, here are the best movies of 2026 so far
items.summary: It's officially halfway through 2026 and the year so far has brought cinematic delights as well as a fair share of silver-screen stinkers. Here are our critics' top picks.

items.title: Coalition focuses fire on Labor, not One Nation, in daily talking points
items.summary: Coalition talking points, obtained by the ABC, make almost no direct mention of One Nation despite the minor party surging in the polls.

items.title: Travel quiz, July 1: Do you have what it takes today?
items.summary: Where would you find the iconic Forrest Gump bench? And do you know where this bridge is?

items.title: Photos reveal room where Australian man allegedly murdered Thai teenager
items.summary: New photographs reveal the cluttered room where 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla was allegedly murdered by Australian man Simon Peter Carman.

items.title: July 1 brings new rules, a tax cut and an increase to paid parental leave
items.summary: A bunch of new rules come into effect today. Find out how they could impact your finances.

items.title: Small NDIS providers and families on edge as registration 'cyclone' looms
items.summary: Uncertainty and confusion are sweeping across the NDIS provider sector with mandatory registration for two categories of providers starting today.

items.title: How to protect your child from head knocks in sport
items.summary: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

items.title: US Supreme Court rejects Donald Trump's bid to restrict birthright citizenship
items.summary: Donald Trump's controversial attempt to restrict birthright citizenship in the US is rejected by the Supreme Court, up-ending the president's hardline agenda on immigration.

items.title: June sees biggest monthly fall in national housing values since 2022, Cotality finds
items.summary: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

items.title: NT residents are the nation's happiest, university study finds
items.summary: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

items.title: Scientists baffled at 60 per cent drop in migrating whale sightings off South West of WA
items.summary: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.

items.title: Menswear brand accused of using AI to 'whitewash' model’s image
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Australian model accuses menswear brand Peter Jackson of using AI to 'whitewash' his image
items.summary: Australian model Elii Emeghebo is pursuing a racial discrimination complaint against suit label Peter Jackson, claiming AI has been used to "whitewash" his image.

items.title: What critics are saying about Legally Blonde prequel Elle
items.summary: Legally Blonde was an overwhelming hit, so what do critics think of the prequel Elle?

items.title: Melbourne childcare provider stripped of federal funding over serious safety breaches
items.summary: The Melbourne childcare provider has been cut off under new powers that allow the federal government to withdraw funding due to safety breaches.

items.title: Anger in Far North Queensland after Hill electorate abolished in redistribution
items.summary: There is widespread anger among voters of an abolished Queensland electorate who will potentially face an 11-hour drive to their local MP's office.

items.title: TGA warns several sexual enhancement supplements contain hidden prescription drugs
items.summary: The TGA issues a warning for consumers using sexual enhancement products after an investigation finds several products contain undeclared prescription drugs that could cause serious side effects.

items.title: Thunderbirds co-captain to lead 'connected' team for Super Netball Grand Final
items.summary: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are looking to win their third premiership in four years, with co-captain Georgie Horjus putting their success down to strong team connections on and off the court.

items.title: Sunraysia Football Netball League adopts new concussion app to improve transparency
items.summary: A sporting league in regional Victoria is the first to adopt a concussion app aimed at improving communication and accountability around concussions at grassroots sports.

items.title: Whyalla steelworks jobs at risk if 'one last crack' to restart blast furnace fails
items.summary: The aging blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks could be permanently shut down before the steelworks transitions to a direct-reduced iron plant and electric arc furnace, placing hundreds of jobs at risk.

items.title: Perth paediatrician Chia Liang Saw accused of child sex offences now facing 53 charges
items.summary: A Perth paediatrician accused of using hidden cameras to record himself abusing children is now facing more than 50 charges involving 10 alleged victims, with a court told further charges are likely to be laid.

items.title: Two deals show Donald Trump's different approaches to key players in Middle East conflicts
items.summary: Two recent Middle East deals, one in Iran and the other in Lebanon, seem at odds with each other, and show how the Trump administration is running different approaches depending on who it is talking to at any given moment.

items.title: SA coroner finds 'no genuine opportunity' to prevent double-fatal Urrbrae crash
items.summary: A South Australian coroner says it is an "unfortunate truth" that there was no way for police to predict or prevent a collision that killed a senior police officer and another woman in 2020.

items.title: Five tonnes of antibiotic florfenicol used last summer to treat disease, Tasmanian salmon industry says
items.summary: More than five tonnes of antibiotics were used to treat a disease that killed 9,000 tonnes of fish last summer, Salmon Tasmania says.

items.title: Ahead of his Origin recall, can Bradman Best be New South Wales's saviour again?
items.summary: In just two matches Bradman Best has earned a reputation as a State of Origin saviour and New South Wales will be desperate for a repeat of those heroics in the decider.

items.title: Fossil kept in drawer for 40 years is Antarctica’s first dinosaur bone
items.summary: A bone found in Antarctica in 1985, and kept in a drawer in England for almost four decades, turns out to belong to one of the largest types of dinosaurs to ever walk the Earth.

items.title: Temporary ban to protect Whyalla's 'significantly low' cuttlefish numbers
items.summary: From Wednesday, a 90-day exclusion will begin at the cuttlefish sanctuary zone between Black Point and Stony Point in South Australia's Upper Spencer Gulf.

items.title: Suspected saltwater crocodile takes dog at Flinders River near Richmond
items.summary: Richmond Shire Mayor John Wharton believes a dog has been taken by a saltwater crocodile in the Flinders River, hundreds of kilometres inland from its usual habitat.

items.title: Nervous wait for snow in NSW alps ahead of school holidays
items.summary: Snow may finally be on the way as NSW ski resorts ride out a slow start to the season.

items.title: Federal MP Josh Burns slams social media giants for antisemitism response at royal commission
items.summary: Federal Victorian MP Josh Burns details an attack on his electorate office where a hammer was used to smash the windows, and the following abuse on social media.

items.title: Watchdog extends prohibition on Emily Lal providing freebirth services
items.summary: Emily Lal, who previously operated online as The Authentic Birthkeeper, will be banned from advertising, providing advice and health services for another three months while the Victorian health watchdog investigates.

items.title: Don't ask me what's culturally appropriate, I don't know either
items.summary: After sending out invitations to a belated elopement party, I was suddenly fielding calls from friends who wanted to wear saris but were worried that it might be disrespectful.

items.title: 'MrBeast model': Is Stefanovic chasing the far right or the algorithm?
items.summary: Why is the former king of Australian breakfast television trading studio lights for ring lights?

items.title: World Cup: Lifeless Germany fall as Paraguay show tenacity to die another day
items.summary: Paraguay's upset penalty-shootout win over Germany at the World Cup, while stunning, was not entirely unexpected, as they showed impressive determination to die another day.

items.title: TPG Capital private equity boss Joel Thickins pleads guilty to drink-driving-related offences
items.summary: Private equity baron Joel Thickins has had his license suspended after pleading guilty to negligent driving while under the influence of alcohol and refusing a breath test.

items.title: Ernst and Young staff sacked after Anthony Albanese's private banking information allegedly breached
items.summary: Two Ernst and Young employees have been sacked after Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking information was allegedly breached while the pair worked for the accounting firm at the Commonwealth Bank.

items.title: Police search for author of threatening letters sent to politicians, religious organisations over 10-year span
items.summary: Police have asked for the community's assistance to identify the author targeting parliamentarians and religious organisations, who goes by 'Scorpio' or 'Bullit'.

items.title: ACCC sues Amazon over alleged unfair conduct for introducing Prime streaming service ads
items.summary: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon over alleged unfair contract terms for its Prime streaming service.

items.title: Retiring Ben Stokes backs Harry Brook as England's next Test captain
items.summary: Ben Stokes says vice-captain Harry Brook has his "100 per cent" support to lead England's Test side, but coach Brendon McCullum is less effusive.

items.title: Jannik Sinner survives Wimbledon scare, Aryna Sabalenka coasts, Naomi Osaka rocks kimono
items.summary: Jannik Sinner is pushed to the brink and Aryna Sabalenka coasts into the second round as both top seeds bounce back from disappointment at the French Open.

items.title: Liberal MP suspended from parliament for 24 hours after QT outburst
items.summary: Liberal MP Garth Hamilton has been suspended from the House of Representatives for 24 hours after an outburst during Question Time.

items.title: Coroner rules hiker's death in Mount Canobolas bushland not suspicious, praises 12-day search
items.summary: The NSW coroner praises emergency services for "persistence" during the 12-day search for a hiker who set off on an ill-fated midnight mountain walk in sub-zero temperatures while wearing sandals.

items.title: Seven Australians in Venezuela request help as earthquake death toll rises
items.summary: Australia is providing "consular assistance" to seven people after Venezuela's two earthquakes, Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong confirms.

items.title: Midwife hit with $70,000 foreign investment tax after moving to Australia from UK
items.summary: A British midwife who migrated to Australia to work at a regional hospital during statewide staff shortages is calling for changes to the law after she was slugged $70,000 by state and federal authorities for the purchase of her home.

items.title: Simpson Desert in bloom but Birdsville's tourism season in limbo
items.summary: Birdsville, a pilgrimage point for outback travellers, navigates the ripple effects of flood, fuel fears and the cancellation of a massive music festival, when the surrounding country has rarely looked so green and alive.

items.title: Worker says Victoria's forestry transition program 'decimated' Gippsland's small towns
items.summary: The Victorian Forestry Transition Program is ending. In the regions that relied on the timber trade, the question is whether it worked.

items.title: Are the wheels falling off the AI investment boom?
items.summary: Overshadowing the tumult that is global politics has been a financial boom beyond almost anything witnessed before, focused entirely on the perceived benefits wrought by a brave new world of AI that threatens to up-end our lives.

items.title: Sensitive documents target of Australia's social media age ban crackdown
items.summary: Labor wants to double the fines for tech giants who refuse to hand over documents that could prove they are failing to enforce Australia’s social media age ban.

items.title: CTE poses a massive dilemma for the AFL. Experts say urgent action is needed to protect players
items.summary: Contact is an essential part of footy, but there is growing evidence it can also be a grave threat to the health and welfare of its players. Experts say the AFL should be acting now to protect current and future players.

items.title: 'Systemic racism' claims identified in buried reef authority review
items.summary: The damning results of a review into the agency tasked with managing the Great Barrier Reef, an area bigger than New Zealand and home to 70 traditional owner groups, has never before seen the light of day.

items.title: Ariane Beeston critiques ballet's toxic culture in new YA novel, Don't Forget to Smile
items.summary: Ariane Beeston, the author of an acclaimed memoir detailing her experience with postpartum psychosis, has penned her first work of fiction, Don't Forget to Smile.

items.title: Lodging too early and other mistakes people make when doing their taxes
items.summary: With side hustles, working from home, crypto investments, renting out rooms and receiving income through digital platforms or apps, experts say people are at greater risk of making errors with their tax.
